subject: Statistics
title: Why We Need Control Groups
description: A 60-second lesson on Why We Need Control Groups.

hook.text: A doctor gives patients a new drug and they improve. Proof it works? Not even close.

buildup.text: Without a control group, you can't tell whether patients improved because of the drug or on their own.

discovery.text: A control group experiences everything except the treatment. The difference between the two groups is the effect.

twist.text: Before control groups, bloodletting was 'proven' medicine. Patients who survived were counted as cures.

climax.text: James Lind's 1747 scurvy trial — comparing citrus to other remedies — was one of the first controlled experiments.

punchline.text: Without a comparison, every treatment 'works.' Controls reveal the truth.

quiz.question: Why are control groups essential in experiments?
options: They show what happens without the treatment, so you can isolate the actual effect
They are only needed in medical research
